summary: Solid two‑set show with flawless Desolation Row and strong Throwing Stones jam
blurb: Features a pristine Desolation Row and a powerful Throwing Stones/NFA stretch in the second set. Jerry’s guitar recovers after early tone issues while Phil endures a broken string but keeps the groove. Overall an all‑around solid performance with good set flow.
review:
On September 6, 1988 at the Capital Centre, the Dead delivered a reliably tight two‑set outing that rewards patient listening. The first set opens with a warm “Jack Straw” and rolls through classics before hitting a standout moment on “Desolation Row,” which many fans describe as flawless – a rare highlight for this era. A brief hiccup in Jerry’s tone early in the night gives way to a confident ride through the “Not Fade Away”‑powered Throwing Stones/NFA wave, where Brent’s keyboards and the dual drummers lock into an infectious groove. The second set’s climax with “The Other One” and a soaring “Throwing Stones” showcases the band’s improvisational chemistry, while the encore “Brokedown Palace” closes on a gentle, reflective note.

While the Miller soundboard capture (gd88-09-06.sbd.miller.27750.sbeok.flacf) offers clear SBD fidelity with only slight crowd bleed, an audience tape (gd88-09-06.aud.ingle.9984.sbeok.shnf) adds a sense of live energy despite its tinny quality and occasional vocal muffs. Together they paint a picture of a solid performance that may lack the fireworks of a legendary night but still provides plenty of moments worth revisiting for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings describe the show as solid with clear positives (flawless Desolation Row, strong Throwing Stones/NFA stretch) and minor negatives limited to recording quality and a brief guitar tone issue. The overall sentiment leans slightly positive across multiple sources, justifying a modest +0.5 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good soundboard source with clear capture, minimal drop‑outs and the only recording praised for its flawless Desolation Row.

key_highlights: Flawless performance of "Desolation Row" in the first set
"Throwing Stones"/"Not Fade Away" wave delivers a powerful second‑set climax
Strong improvisation on "The Other One" and seamless segues throughout
Overall solid set flow with no glaring weak spots

band_performance.Jerry: Early in the show his guitar tone wavered and vocals were muffled, but he recovers and rides the NFA wave effortlessly later.
band_performance.Phil: Suffers a broken string on bass during the first set yet maintains a solid groove throughout.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums are described as quiet and breathy during the space segment, contributing to a more subdued percussion texture.
band_performance.Billy: Percussion follows the same quiet, breathy character in space, keeping the overall rhythm low‑key.
